title: Comments
text: Comments Uniface recognizes the first semicolon ( ; ) on a line that occurs outside a string constant, as the start of a comment. All the remaining characters, with the exception of a continuation marker ( %/ ), on the line are treated as comment. The following ProcScript examples shows comment between lines of ProcScript code: function myProc ; This ProcScript function logs $procerror if ($procerror < 0) putmess "Error - $procerror = %%$procerror" endif done Copy The following ProcScript examples shows comment after a ProcScript instruction: function myProc if ($procerror < 0) ; if an error occurred putmess "Error - $procerror = %%$procerror" endif done Copy Was this topic helpful?
